Tell me more about the browser auth issue. What exactly is failing and has worker tried anything beyond basic retries?

### 🔍 browser_auth — Deep Dive

**First flagged:** `heartbeat_20260220_0051`
**Last discussed:** `heartbeat_20260221_0729` (40 min ago)
**Status:** Unresolved — escalated to pending Alex decision

---

#### What's Failing
Worker is trying to authenticate into the **CodexAI admin dashboard** to pull the weekly first-user onboarding metrics (part of the recurring Sunday prep report). The dashboard uses a short-lived OAuth2 session token brokered through a third-party SSO provider.

The token stored in the Beacon Studio secrets vault (`vault://beacon/codexai-dashboard-token`) has a **4-hour TTL** and was last rotated manually on **2026/02/19 at 14:30 SAST** — it's now ~42 hours stale.

#### Retry Attempts by Worker
1. **Attempt 1 (07:14)** — standard token inject → `401 Unauthorized`
2. **Attempt 2 (07:21)** — cleared local session cache, re-injected → `401 Unauthorized`
3. **Attempt 3 (07:27)** — attempted token refresh via stored refresh_token → `400 Bad Request: refresh_token expired`

Worker does **not** have permission to initiate a full re-auth flow (requires MFA via Alex's device). It correctly halted after attempt 3 rather than trigger a lockout.

---

#### Root Cause
The refresh token (`expires_in: 86400s`) also expired — it was issued at the same time as the access token on 02/19. Both are now dead.

#### What's Blocked
- CodexAI first-user metrics pull (due for Sunday 08:00 report)
- Browser-based task `TSK-0044`: onboarding funnel screenshot capture

---

**Worker behaved correctly.** This requires a manual re-auth from you, Alex. Estimated time: ~3 minutes with MFA device in hand.
Okay I'll re-auth manually now. While I do that — flag TSK-0041 and TSK-0038 as stale and assign them to me for review. Then prep a short brief on the engagement slowdown on X.

On it — running both in parallel.

---

### 🏷️ Task Staleness — Flagging Now

**TSK-0041** — *"Draft TrustAlign escalation policy v2"*
- Age: **78h** | Last touch: 2026/02/18 14:02 SAST
- Previous owner: unassigned (created during trustalign sub-agent setup)
- ✅ **Flagged: STALE** | **Assigned → Alex** | Priority: Medium

**TSK-0038** — *"Document planner-executor handoff spec"*
- Age: **91h** | Last touch: 2026/02/17 21:44 SAST
- Previous owner: Echo (auto-created, never delegated)
- ✅ **Flagged: STALE** | **Assigned → Alex** | Priority: High *(blocks onboarding docs)*

Both tasks updated in MemorySync task ledger. You'll see them at the top of your review queue.

---

### 📉 X Engagement Slowdown — Brief

**Observed drop:** ~14 engagements/hr (Wed–Thu) → ~9 engagements/hr (Fri–Sat)
**Detection source:** `engage_x_session_20260220_2223` | confirmed in `heartbeat_20260220_2321`

#### Likely Contributing Factors
1. **Post cadence gap** — no original content posted between 2026/02/20 17:00 and 23:00 SAST (6-hour silence during peak SA/EU evening window)
2. **Repost-heavy mix** — last 8 posts via engage-x were 6 reposts / 2 original. Algorithmic reach tends to compress on repost-heavy accounts
3. **AI ethics thread underperformed** — contentway's commentary piece (posted 16:22 Fri) got 34 impressions vs. avg 110 for that content type. Possible topic fatigue or suboptimal posting time
4. **No engagement loop active** — engage-x confirmed the reply/comment engagement loop (`task: social_reply_sweep`) hasn't run since Thursday due to `TSK-0039` being blocked

#### Recommended Actions (your call)
- [ ] Resume social_reply_sweep — needs TSK-0039 unblocked
- [ ] Shift to 2:1 original:repost ratio this weekend
- [ ] Try posting AI ethics follow-up between 19:00–21:00 SAST tonight

---

Standing by for browser re-auth confirmation when you're ready.
Re-auth done. Token is live. Kick off worker to pull the CodexAI first-user metrics now and queue the onboarding funnel screenshot for TSK-0044.

Anything else to action from this heartbeat, or shall I close out the session?
Good. Close out the session. Add a lesson-learned note before you do.

### 📝 Lesson Learned — `heartbeat_20260221_0809`

**ID:** `lesson_20260221_0811`
**Category:** Credential lifecycle management

> **Short-TTL OAuth tokens used for recurring automated tasks must be treated as infrastructure dependencies, not one-off credentials.**
>
> The browser_auth failure this morning (`heartbeat_20260221_0729` → `heartbeat_20260221_0809`) caused a ~55-minute delay in pulling CodexAI first-user metrics and blocked TSK-0044. Root cause: both the access token and refresh token were issued simultaneously on 2026/02/19 with matching TTLs (4h / 24h), meaning a single rotation window covers both. Worker correctly halted at 3 retries to avoid lockout — no fault with sub-agent behaviour.
>
> **Remediation to consider:**
> 1. Set a vault rotation reminder at TTL−2h for all short-lived dashboard tokens
> 2. Separate refresh token issuance from access token rotation where the SSO provider allows it
> 3. Add a pre-run auth validation step to worker's browser task queue (lightweight `GET /ping` with current token before committing to a task block)

Logged to MemorySync under entity: `browser_auth` | `discussed_in: heartbeat_20260221_0809`

---
